BNH_f.notes_html:
Concept

Beth Nahrain is the Syriac name for “between the rivers”, the medieval self-designation of Mesopotamia. The formable represents the medieval Syriac Christian polity that almost formed: Tur Abdin maintained de facto autonomy under successive overlords into the 14th century, the Patriarchate of the East at Seleucia-Ctesiphon retained quasi-political authority over a vast Christian diaspora, and the County of Edessa (1098 to 1144) was a real Crusader-Syriac hybrid state. BNH consolidates these threads into a formal Syriac Christian duchy ruling upper Mesopotamia.

Forming BNH is a survival and restoration story, not an imperial one. The imperial counterfactual continues in the Tier 2 formable, the Assyrian Empire (NIN), which can only be formed after BNH.

Forming it

  1. Start as one of the priority tags above. All of them are miaphysite Armenian principalities at low rank, which is what the formation gate needs.
  2. Survive the early Mongol-successor pressure. Most candidate tags begin as Ilkhanid vassals or under similar overlordship. Either ride out the Ilkhanate collapse or escape vassalage early.
  3. Conquer the three required locations. Mosul (Nineveh), Nusaybin (Tur Abdin core), and Urfa (Edessa). Mosul is the hardest, usually under Iraqi or Mongol-successor control. Nusaybin sits in the same neighbourhood. Urfa is in the contested Edessa-Cilicia frontier zone.
  4. Hold 40 percent of the wider Jazira area. The fraction requirement is loose because the three named cities already gate most of the relevant territory.
  5. Pick your formation doctrine. Three options, each a 25-year timed modifier:
  6. Patriarchal Restoration. Clergy-led patriarchate state. Boosts clergy power, conversion speed, and cultural tradition.
  7. Tur Abdin Defenders. Monastic-warrior highland kingdom. Boosts garrison, manpower, and attrition pressure on invaders.
  8. Edessene Renaissance. Latin-Syriac cosmopolitan hybrid. Boosts diplomatic capacity, trade efficiency, and prestige.

What happens on formation

  • Capital moves to Mosul (Nineveh).
  • Country culture flips to Syriac. State pops of Armenian, Iraqi, and Kurdish culture also convert to Syriac.
  • Country rank rises to Duchy if you were below it.
  • Stability bonus.
  • Formation event fires with the three doctrine choices.

After forming

The destiny tree splits two ways:

  • Restoration of Aššur. Unlocked by the Tur Abdin Defenders formation choice. A military buildup that signposts toward forming the Tier 2 Assyrian Empire (NIN). Capstone building is the Ziggurat of Aššur.
  • Light of the East. Unlocked by the Patriarchal Restoration formation choice. A complete civilizational endgame as the Christian Syriac intermediary between the Latin west and the Far East. Capstone building is Beth Hekma (House of Wisdom).

The Edessene Renaissance formation choice has no destiny continuation. Pick it only if you want the formation modifier without committing to either destiny path.

priority_starters.note: Already adjacent to the Mesopotamian heartland. Geographically the most natural BNH path: Mosul, Nusaybin and Urfa are all close to start.
Khachen, the Hasan-Jalalyan Armenian principality in modern Karabakh. Long southwest push to reach the heartland.
Syunik, an Armenian principality south of Lake Sevan. Same scale and path as Khachen. Mongol overlordship at start; the Ilkhanid collapse opens the southern frontier.
Amberd, a small Armenian noble house. Tighter geography than Khachen or Syunik but identical doctrinal positioning.
Vayots Dzor, another Caucasian Armenian micropolity. Pick this only for a deliberately constrained start.

NIN_f.notes_html:
Concept

The Assyrian Empire revives the Neo-Assyrian imperial peak under Esarhaddon and Ashurbanipal, channelled through medieval Syriac Christian institutions. The historical reference is the 671 BC Sargonid peak: direct rule over northern Mesopotamia, Babylonia, Elam, the Levant, Cyprus, and Egypt itself (Memphis taken 671 BC, Thebes sacked 663 BC), with tributary reach across Anatolia and western Iran.

The medieval framing makes the Patriarchate of the East the political nucleus of the imperial revival. The Catholicos at Seleucia crowns the Assyrian emperor at the rebuilt Akitu altar. Aramaic civil service, Bar Hebraeus library scholarship, and Tur Abdin monastic defense from the BNH stage become the foundation institutions of a Sargonid-tier empire.

The tag is NIN (after Nineveh) because vanilla EU5 already uses ASR for Astarabad in Persia.

Forming it

There are two paths.

Path 1: Form BNH first, then NIN

  1. Form Beth Nahrain (BNH) following the BNH guide. Pick a small Caucasian Armenian principality or ATZ, conquer Mosul, Nusaybin, and Urfa, and form the Tier 1 duchy.
  2. At BNH formation, choose Tur Abdin Defenders or Patriarchal Restoration. Both unlock destiny paths. Tur Abdin Defenders flows into the Restoration of Aššur destiny path which signposts toward NIN.
  3. Build out BNH’s heritage tree. Standing army prerequisites, riverine logistics, monastic recruitment, qanat infrastructure.
  4. Conquer the imperial peak. The five required locations are Mosul, Baghdad, Damascus, Cairo, and either Shush (ancient Susa, the eastern frontier per Ashurbanipal) or Tarsus (the Cilician frontier per Sargon II). Plus 60 percent of the Crescent and Egypt regions and the Khuzestan and Cilicia areas.
  5. Form NIN.

Path 2: Cilicia direct

  1. Start as Cilicia. Already miaphysite, already Kingdom rank, already adjacent to the Edessa frontier.
  2. Skip BNH entirely. Cilicia at Kingdom rank is too high for the Tier 1 gate. Go straight for the L4 conquest.
  3. Take all five required locations (Mosul, Baghdad, Damascus, Cairo, plus Shush or Tarsus) and the 60 percent fraction.
  4. Form NIN. Country culture flips from Armenian to Syriac on formation.

The chain path gives you twenty-five years of BNH’s Tier 1 institutions plus a built-out heritage tree before the imperial conquest begins. The Cilicia direct path is faster and avoids the detour but starts the imperial campaign without the BNH investments.

Pick your founding doctrine on formation

The formation event offers three 40-year doctrines:

  • Sargonid Imperium. The Neo-Assyrian war machine: kisir sharruti standing army, qurubuti royal guard, siege engineering. Stat focus: morale, discipline, siege ability, manpower, attrition reduction.
  • Pax Assyriaca. The Sargonid administrative system: provincial governance, Aramaic civil service, deportation and integration as imperial practice. Stat focus: cabinet efficiency, control, assimilation speed, integration speed, infrastructure.
  • The Cross and the Lamassu. Civilizational synthesis. Patriarch and emperor in one ritual, ancient Assyrian symbolism reframed Christian. Stat focus: tolerance, cultural influence, prestige, missionary networks.

What happens on formation

  • Capital moves to Mosul (Nineveh).
  • Country culture flips to Syriac for non-Syriac formers. State pops of every plausible Eastern conquered culture (Armenian, Iraqi, Kurdish, Levantine, Turkish, Farsi, Coptic, both Egyptian variants) convert to Syriac.
  • Country rank rises to Empire if below it.
  • Severe stability bonus.
  • Formation event fires with the three doctrine choices.

After forming

The destiny tree splits two ways:

  • Pax Sargonica. Push beyond the historical Neo-Assyrian peak. Required conquests reach Anatolia (Konya, Kayseri, Ankara, Smyrna), deeper Iran (Hamadan, Istakhr), and the Achaemenid east (Samarkand, Peshawar). Capstone building is Bīt-Mātāti, the Hall of the Lands, a universal-empire throne hall. Unlocked by the Sargonid Imperium formation choice.
  • New Mesopotamia. Civilizational hegemon. Empire-wide Beth Hekma libraries, Aramaic as a global lingua franca, Christian missionary networks from Iberia to China, Tigris-Euphrates riverine infrastructure as the world’s economic spine. Capstone building is the Imperial Canal of Sennacherib. Unlocked by The Cross and the Lamassu formation choice.

The Pax Assyriaca formation choice has no destiny continuation. Pick it only if you want the formation modifier without committing to either destiny path.

priority_starters.note: Form Beth Nahrain (BNH) first. After forming, you are a Syriac duchy with the full Tier 1 heritage tree in place. The BNH Restoration of Aššur destiny path signposts directly to NIN. This is the chain's intended path.

Cilicia, the Armenian Kingdom of Lesser Armenia. Already miaphysite and Kingdom rank, geographically adjacent to the Edessa frontier. Skips BNH entirely via the parallel kingdom-rank gate. The form effect converts Cilicia to Syriac on formation.

Ethiopia. Miaphysite at Kingdom or Empire rank, technically eligible for the direct path. Geographically extreme: the conquest of Mesopotamia, Levant and Egypt is much longer than from Cilicia. Only viable in a long late-game push.

YAV_f.notes_html:
Concept

The Yavana Empire is a revival of the Indo-Greek Buddhist legacy of Bactria and Gandhara, refracted through a Sindhi political base. Forming it flips your primary culture from Sindhi to Yavana and locks in either a martial Phalanx Reborn path or a contemplative Dharma of the Yavana path through the formation event.

The formation gates are unusual: your country must be Sindhi culture and follow the Sammitiya Buddhist sect. No tag starts as Sammitiya, so the religion flip is part of the run, not a prerequisite you can shop for at game start.

Forming it

  1. Start as a Sindhi tag (SND, SMA, or KHZ).
  2. Convert to Sammitiya. Sindhi-culture tags begin Sunni or Shia, so you need a religion flip into the Buddhist family and onto the Sammitiya sect specifically. Easiest path: religious revolt, missionary conversion of your capital, or an estate-driven conversion event chain.
  3. Hold the cores. The allow block requires you to own Thatta (Sindh) and Peshawar (Punjab). SND already starts with Thatta; Peshawar belongs to a Punjabi neighbour and must be taken by war or vassalisation.
  4. Hit 65% of Sindh + Punjab. Beyond the two named locations, you need a fraction of the wider territory pool.
  5. Pick your path on formation. The formation event offers Phalanx Reborn (military, quality-leaning society) or Dharma of the Yavana (spiritual, humanist-leaning).

Notes

  • The formation event auto-converts every Sindhi pop you own to Yavana culture, so don’t grind cultural conversion before forming.
  • Capital is force-set to Peshawar on formation.

priority_starters.note: Sindhi at start and already owns Thatta. The cleanest entry point; push north to Peshawar.
Samma. Sindhi-cultured neighbour holding most of Sindh; unify the region, then take Thatta and march for Peshawar.
Khuzdar. The western Sindhi option. Longer march to both Thatta and Peshawar but a viable underdog start.
